data.description:
# Committed store compatibility record with a fail-closed capability gate

Introduce a committed store-level compatibility record (`format_version` +
`required_capabilities`) that a v1.0 binary reads before any mutating or
externally-publishing operation, failing closed on an unknown version or required
capability while keeping reads and diagnostics available. (Readiness Finding 2 —
verified VALID against `src/rebar/_store/ensures.py`, `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py`,
`src/rebar/_commands/{compact,fsck,init}.py`, `docs/migrations.md`.)

## Why

rebar's migration mechanism is an idempotent desired-state ensure-registry, not an
ordered ledger: `run_ensures` runs every unit under the write lock, converts
exceptions to `failed`, and **returns normally** (`src/rebar/_store/ensures.py`
docstring "Never raises"; init/boot never abort). Event compatibility is
preserve-and-ignore: unknown event types are skipped in replay
(`reducer/_replay.py:195-196`) and omitted from the compaction fold but kept on
disk (`_commands/compact.py:179-195`), and `fsck` emits an informational WARN that
does not increment `issue_count` or stop operation (`_commands/fsck.py:229-252`,
which itself notes "a reconcile host on an old binary may push stale state").

This forward-compat is correct for *optional additive* data — it prevents an old
compactor from deleting new bytes. But there is **no committed store-format-version
or required-capability record** (confirmed absent; `SCHEMA_VERSION` is a stamp,
never compared to fail closed). So an old binary computes state that omits the
unknown event and can then emit a new event, compact, evaluate close/verify policy,
or push a stale field to Jira from that incomplete state. `docs/migrations.md`
explicitly files the "A-tier ledger + committed store-format version that fails
closed" under future work. **The reader must ship in v1.0**: an already-installed
v1.0 binary cannot be taught later to inspect a record whose existence and
semantics were not defined in v1.0 — so to ever mark a store as requiring a
post-v1.0 capability, v1.0 must already know how to detect and refuse unknown
required capabilities.

## What

Adopt git's repository-format-version pattern
(`core.repositoryformatversion` + `extensions.*`: an implementation that does not
understand the advertised format or a required extension must not operate, because
continuing can produce incorrect results or lose data), and git's staged rollout
(teach readers first, make writing opt-in, default later):

- Add a committed store-level record, minimally:
  `{"format_version": 1, "required_capabilities": []}`.
- Add a compatibility check that runs **before** any operation that mutates the
  store or publishes derived state: event append, transition, claim, compaction /
  snapshot rebuild, delete, archive, Jira push / reconcile, MCP write tools,
  automatic repair, and migration execution.
- On an unknown `format_version` or an unrecognised required capability: allow
  `fsck`, diagnostics, backup, and explicit read-only inspection; report the
  unsupported version/capability; block the mutating/publishing operations before
  they acquire side effects (fail-closed).
- Keep the ensure-registry for convergent check-then-act repairs. Do **not** build
  the ordered ledger in this story — only the compatibility record + reader gate.
  (An ordered once-only ledger is a separate future concern for non-convergent,
  irreversible migrations.)

## Scope

- New module under `src/rebar/_store/` for the compatibility record + gate; wired
  into the write/compact/push/reconcile/MCP-write/repair entry points.
- `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py` — relate `format_version` to the existing
  preserve-and-ignore `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` machinery (optional-additive stays
  preserve-and-ignore; required-capability is the new fail-closed path).
- `docs/migrations.md` — document the record, the fail-closed semantics, and the
  optional-additive vs required-capability distinction.
- Interface tests extending `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_event_schema_forward_compat.py`.
- Out of scope: the ordered migration ledger; changing any existing event-replay
  behaviour for currently-known types.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A committed store compatibility record (`format_version` +
  `required_capabilities`) is created at init and read by a gate function.
- [ ] The gate blocks mutation, compaction, repair, push, and reconcile before any
  side effect when `format_version` is unknown or a required capability is
  unrecognised (demonstrated by Experiment 2A: an old-build reconcile/compact/write
  exits before producing a side effect against a store requiring a new capability).
- [ ] `fsck`, diagnostics, backup, and read-only inspection remain available under
  an unknown version/capability and clearly report the unsupported version/capability.
- [ ] Optional additive (unknown-but-not-required) events retain today's
  preserve-and-ignore behaviour — the existing forward-compat contract tests still
  pass, and a new test proves a *required* capability fails closed while an
  *optional* additive event does not.
- [ ] `docs/migrations.md` documents the compatibility record and the fail-closed
  gate, including the deployment-order rationale (why the reader ships in v1.0).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass, and the change lands via the
  Gerrit two-vote gate.
data.title: Committed store compatibility record with a fail-closed capability gate
